title: CraftSound
description: Spice up your Minecraft experience with 39 unique sound when crafting different items!
body:
want to spice up your Minecraft experience? Here! crafting with sound adds a lot of juice to the game. All the sounds are vanilla sound just play when crafting with 40 sounds. such as wood, stone, iron and etc. for EVERY ITEM that can be crafting in vanilla! (currently support 1.15 - 1.21.4) (Not supporting modded items)

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/4iH_uZcYaoU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

the current version is 3.13 please update if you are not using latest version.
Notice: You can download this CraftSound for Minecraft 1.14 and older version here Google Drive. 
I no longer support them, use at your own risk, and versions 1.15 - 1.19 are no longer receive content. 

#### [External download link](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/11YuGhnMfQPtrdSaCaCk_DnquPjTk568r 'title') Contained achieved and some older versions.


### How to adjust sounds volume 
The crafting sounds is tide to Players channel in Music&Sound options menu.You can tune it down as you desire.

### How to open personal settings
Simply enter the command "/trigger pbbs.cs.trigger set 1" in this menu will contain. (the commands might not be executable if administrator prohibit this command or edited the datapack)

**[​Mute]** (true or false)\ 
You won't hear any sounds creating by this datapack. Use when you dislike this datapack but playing the host that have this installed and you don't want to turn off players sounds entirely. note that you still do crafting sounds to other players.

**[​Chance]**\
Control chance that crafting will make sounds (default is 100%) you can set it to 
10%, 20%, 25%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 75%, 80%, 90% and 100%. This option only effect you. 

### How to open datapack menu (Administrator needed)
Simply enter the command "/function playbabe:menu/craftsound/open" in this menu will contain.

**[​ForceInstall]**\
in above Craftsound version 3.0, I implement a check to my datapack. it will check the version of the scoreboard it install inside the save and decide to install if there is none, do noting if version iscorrect and warn you if the version is incorrect. The last will only appear when you in appropriatelyupdate the datapack (see how to update). in the case you are quite lazy to do the whole processyou can force install the datapack. but it not guarantee that the datapack will work without bug.

**[​UnInstall]**\
straightforward, plz do it before update or change the Craftsound versionIMPORTANT HOW TO UPDATE!1) run command "/function playbabe:menu/craftsound/open"2) an options message should appear, left-click at [​uninstall] message 3) the datapack should uninstall and disable itself.4) delete old version datapack in world folder where it installed5) download latest version and install it

### IMPORTANT HOW TO UPDATE!
1) run command "/function playbabe:menu/craftsound/open"
2) an options message should appear, left-click at [​uninstall] message
3) the datapack should uninstall and disable itself.
4) delete old version datapack in world folder where it installed
5) download latest version and install it

### Quick Q&A
Q: My datapack or one of my plugin create custom recipe that make it own sound, does it impact anything?\
A: If the recipe where craft by crafting table or inventory, yes, CS will play sound anyway regarding the context.

Q: Is it possible to disable CS sound for specific recipe ?\
A: no, just specific item (see how to edit my datapack below).

Q: Datapack is not worked on forge's server or forge of forge's server\
A: Some reported to me that unpack the zip file worked as a fix. and other datapack seems to have same problem but tho randomly.

### What's next ?
implement more sounds variant, adjust the current one to be less annoying and more subtle. unfortunately, the datapack are aimed to not using any custom sounds (ie. resource pack independent) so I don't have a large sounds pool to choose from.

### Customization 
#### How to edit sounds
locate and go to "\data\playbabe\functions\craftsound\sounds\small" inside datapack folder, it contain sounds command respect to the items. 
#### How to disable sound for certain item
Look into "auto_install.mcfunction" and find the item you want to remove. Remove the scoreboard object associate to it both in the game will disable sound for assigned item. but this will not prevent future installation to bring it back. (if demanded i will imprement a proper feature for this)
